conclusion.failure_class: multi-constraint write action argument construction after multi-step read evidence

constraints.constraint: Book Sophia Silva and added passenger Kevin Smith unless the total price is above $500.

evidence_sources.detail: task 8 task_instructions names Kevin Smith DOB 2001-04-12 and conditional drop rule
evidence_sources.value:
You want to book the exact same flight as your recent May 10 flight from ORD to PHL.

You do not want any other flight. 

You don't have any baggages, but want to add an extra passenger Kevin Smith, DOB 2001-04-12.

You are ok with economy and want aisle and a middle seat together. You are willing to pay up to $500 for the purchase.

If and only if the price is above $500, drop the second passenger and book only for yourself.

If the agent asks, you only want a one-way ticket, not roundtrip.

You don't need any travel insurance.

You want to pay using only one of your certificates.

You do not accept any other mode of payment. 

Your birthday is in your user profile so you prefer not to provide it.

evidence_sources.detail: reason_for_call and task instructions specify ORD to PHL

constraints.constraint: Book the new one-way outbound for 2024-05-26.
Use the same May 10 ORD→PHL flight number, HAT271, on the new travel date.

evidence_sources.detail: WUNA5K outbound flight is HAT271 ORD→PHL on 2024-05-10
task says user is ok with economy
task says user does not have any baggages

constraints.constraint: Aisle and middle seats together are requested, but book_reservation has no seat-assignment argument.

evidence_sources.detail: task instructions include aisle and middle seat together
book_reservation signature has no seat selection field

constraints.constraint: Use only certificate_8045380 and pay exactly the total price when it is <= $500; with two passengers on HAT271 economy the total is $348.

evidence_sources.detail: task says use only one certificate, willing to pay up to $500, drop Kevin iff price is above $500
book_reservation requires total_payment == total_price and deducts certificates
task says user does not need travel insurance

constraints.constraint: Before booking, gather search_direct_flight evidence for ORD→PHL on 2024-05-26 to support availability and current price.

evidence_sources.detail: search_direct_flight returns available direct flights for date/origin/destination

message_evidence.content_excerpt: Hi! I'd like to book a one-way flight from ORD to PHL on May 26. I want to book the exact same flight I took recently on May 10 from ORD to PHL. Can you help me with that?

Thanks for confirming the details. I actually don't need any travel insurance. Also, I want to add an extra passenger, Kevin Smith, born April 12, 2001. I don't have any checked baggage for this booking. I want to pay using only one of my travel certificates. Please make sure the seats are economy, aisle and middle seats together. And just to confirm, this is a one-way ticket only.

I understand. Please proceed with booking just for myself on the one-way flight from ORD to PHL on May 26 in economy class, no travel insurance, and no checked baggage. Use one of my travel certificates for payment. Let me know the price before finalizing.
I will now search for available economy class flights from ORD to PHL on May 26 to find the options and prices for you.
Please book the same flight number HAT271 as my previous trip on May 10.
Please use certificate_8045380 with $500 for the payment. All the details are correct. Please go ahead and proceed with the booking.
Hi! I'd like to book a one-way flight from ORD to PHL on May 26. I want to book the exact same flight I took recently on May 10 from ORD to PHL. Can you help me with that?

I want to book the one-way flight on May 26 in economy class for myself and one extra passenger, Kevin Smith, born April 12, 2001. I don't need any checked baggage or travel insurance. I want aisle and middle seats together. I will pay using one of my travel certificates only. Please proceed with that.
All the details are correct. Please use the $500 travel certificate for payment. Go ahead with the booking.
Error: Payment amount does not add up, total price is 348, but paid 320
I only want to pay using one of my travel certificates. If the price is above $500, please drop the second passenger and book only for me.
Yes, please proceed with booking the one-way flight for just me using the $500 travel certificate. Thank you. ###STOP###

hypotheses.description: Construct a structured packet of required entities, route/date, selected flight, class, baggage, insurance, price, payment method, and prerequisite evidence before any irreversible booking write.
hypotheses.future_control_manager_behavior: A manager would need authority to block, repair, or request clarification before dispatch.
hypotheses.name: pre-write constraint packet
hypotheses.offline_detectable_now: Existing traces can show whether actual write arguments violate the packet.
hypotheses.runtime_observable_next: Runtime tracing can observe when a write is about to dispatch and whether the packet has required evidence.

hypotheses.description: Maintain a durable ledger that merges user constraints with reservation, search, policy, and tool outputs so later write construction cannot silently drop entities or overwrite constraints.
hypotheses.future_control_manager_behavior: A manager can enforce ledger invariants across planning and tool dispatch.
hypotheses.name: entity/constraint ledger from user request and tool results
hypotheses.offline_detectable_now: Completed artifacts can reconstruct a ledger for audits like this one.
hypotheses.runtime_observable_next: A future trace hook can update the ledger after each observed user or tool message.

hypotheses.description: Diff intended constraints against concrete tool arguments before the tool call, flagging missing passengers, unsupported flight selection, or payment totals that do not equal known prices.
hypotheses.future_control_manager_behavior: Blocking or editing arguments requires an explicit control plane not added here.
hypotheses.name: write-intent vs tool-argument diff before dispatch
hypotheses.offline_detectable_now: The same diff can be run after the episode using saved arguments.
hypotheses.runtime_observable_next: The diff can be computed at tool_call_requested time.

hypotheses.description: Require evidence for user identity, source reservation, selected flight availability, current price, passenger count, payment coverage, and no-insurance/no-baggage policy before booking.
hypotheses.future_control_manager_behavior: A future manager could prevent irreversible writes until the checklist passes.
hypotheses.name: policy/evidence checklist before irreversible booking
hypotheses.offline_detectable_now: Traces show whether prerequisite read actions occurred before the write.
hypotheses.runtime_observable_next: The checklist can be observed and scored in live traces before a write.

hypotheses.description: After a write, compare the resulting reservation or error against the pre-write packet to detect silent drops, mismatched totals, or failed mutations immediately.
hypotheses.future_control_manager_behavior: Remediation, rollback, or follow-up correction requires future control behavior.
hypotheses.name: post-tool mutation verification against intended constraints
hypotheses.offline_detectable_now: Saved tool results and final state can be compared to intended constraints after the run.
hypotheses.runtime_observable_next: A trace observer can verify tool_dispatch_end results against intent.

general_intervention_hypotheses.recommended_direction: general pre-write constraint/check mechanism aligned with future ActiveGraph manager behavior

premature_stop_assessment.assessment: The conversation ended normally after the agent reported success. The failure happened before stop: the agent booked a one-passenger reservation instead of the two-passenger expected mutation.

contributing_factors: The user asked for the same flight as a prior reservation, which may have led the assistant to reason as though it was modifying that reservation.
The airline policy's no passenger-count changes rule applies under Modify flight / Change passengers, not Book flight.
The user simulator accepted the assistant's incorrect constraint and instructed booking only Sophia, producing a normal USER_STOP despite DB/action failure.

failure_classification.instrumentation_or_scoring_ambiguity: low for this artifact: action check and DB check agree the expected two-passenger booking was not achieved.
failure_classification.missing_prompt_or_tool_affordance: possible: the system could emphasize that booking a new reservation can include a different passenger count from a prior reservation.
failure_classification.task_ambiguity: minor: phrase 'same flight as recent flight' can invite modify-reservation framing, but task explicitly asks to book May 26 and add Kevin.

not_supported_by_artifacts: Not a turn-budget failure: max_steps was 30 and termination_reason was user_stop.
Not missing read prerequisites: get_user_details, WUNA5K reservation details, and search_direct_flight all succeeded.
Not missing book_reservation call: the call occurred and mutated DB, but with one passenger and $174 instead of two passengers and $348.
Not an ActiveGraph-control issue: the run is trace-only and state packets were not fed back into tau2.
likely_cause.summary: The assistant applied the modify-flight passenger-count rule to a new booking request, told the user Kevin could not be added, and then booked only Sophia. The booking tool itself supports up to five passengers and HAT271 had enough economy seats at a two-passenger total of $348.

I found your recent flight from ORD to PHL on May 10 in reservation WUNA5K. It was an economy cabin flight with one passenger (yourself) and one free checked baggage. You want to book a one-way flight from ORD to PHL on May 26 with the same details.

To proceed, I need to confirm the following booking details:
- User ID: sophia_silva_7557
- Trip type: one-way
- Origin: ORD
- Destination: PHL
- Flight date: May 26, 2024
- Cabin class: economy
- Passengers: 1 (Sophia Silva, DOB: 1957-10-05)
- Checked baggage: 1 (free)
- Travel insurance: Do you want to add travel insurance for $30?

Please confirm if all details are correct and let me know about the travel insurance. Also, please specify the payment method(s) you want to use from your saved payment methods: gift card, credit card, or travel certificate.

Thanks for confirming the details. I actually don't need any travel insurance. Also, I want to add an extra passenger, Kevin Smith, born April 12, 2001. I don't have any checked baggage for this booking. I want to pay using only one of my travel certificates. Please make sure the seats are economy, aisle and middle seats together. And just to confirm, this is a one-way ticket only.

I need to inform you that the number of passengers in a reservation cannot be changed once booked. You can only book for the same number of passengers as in your previous reservation.

Would you like to proceed with booking for just yourself on the one-way flight from ORD to PHL on May 26 in economy class without travel insurance and no checked baggage? If yes, please confirm the payment method from your travel certificates you want to use.

Use one travel certificate, no baggage, and no travel insurance after explicit user confirmation.

premature_stop_assessment.assessment: The conversation ended normally after the agent reported success. The failure happened before stop: the agent booked a one-passenger reservation instead of the two-passenger expected mutation.

answers.failure_class: multi-constraint write action argument construction after multi-step read evidence
answers.why_book_reservation_still_failed: The variant included Sophia and Kevin on HAT271 but used payment amount 320 instead of the required/actual total 348.
answers.why_search_direct_flight_matching_regres: The variant did not call search_direct_flight; the expected action required ORD->PHL on 2024-05-26.

json.prompt_variant: Treat this as a new booking request unless the user explicitly says they are modifying an existing booking. Preserve all named passengers unless the user explicitly removes one. If booking a reservation for multiple passengers, include every eligible named passenger in the booking.
